metadata.title: HTA Body Requirements — Evidence Standards for Reimbursement
metadata.description: Detailed evidence requirements for major Health Technology Assessment bodies. Used by NIX AI payer gap analysis to identify missing evidence for reimbursement.

NICE.full_name: National Institute for Health and Care Excellence

icer_thresholds.end_of_life: Up to £50,000 per QALY with life-extending criteria
icer_thresholds.hst: Up to £100,000 per QALY for highly specialised technologies
icer_thresholds.severity_modifier: Up to 3.2x weighting for most severe conditions (QALY shortfall ≥18 years)

clinical_effectiveness.preferred_evidence: Systematic review of RCTs with meta-analysis
clinical_effectiveness.comparator: NHS standard of care (active comparator preferred)
endpoints_required: Overall survival or equivalent clinical outcome
data_quality: RCTs with active comparator strongly preferred
Network meta-analysis acceptable when no head-to-head data
Real-world evidence can supplement but not replace RCT data

cost_effectiveness.time_horizon: Lifetime (must capture all relevant costs and outcomes)
cost_effectiveness.utility_measurement: EQ-5D-5L mapped to EQ-5D-3L value set (2022 update: EQ-5D-5L value set preferred)
cost_sources: PSSRU Unit Costs of Health and Social Care
sensitivity_analyses: Scenario analyses for key structural assumptions

budget_impact.population_estimates: UK eligible patient numbers with uptake curve

common_gaps_in_protocols: No EQ-5D quality of life measurement
Placebo comparator instead of active standard of care
No head-to-head comparison with NHS-relevant comparator
Insufficient follow-up duration for lifetime extrapolation
Missing healthcare resource use data collection
No pre-specified subgroup for UK/European population
Surrogate endpoints without validated link to OS/QALYs
No patient-reported outcomes mapped to utilities
recent_updates: 2025: Updated severity modifier replacing end-of-life criteria
2024: EQ-5D-5L value set now preferred over mapped EQ-5D-3L
2024: Enhanced real-world evidence framework for post-approval commitments
2023: Managed access agreements and commercial arrangements framework updated

IQWiG.full_name: Institut für Qualität und Wirtschaftlichkeit im Gesundheitswesen

clinical_effectiveness.preferred_evidence: RCTs vs. appropriate comparator therapy (ACT) as defined by G-BA
clinical_effectiveness.comparator: Appropriate Comparator Therapy (ACT) — defined by Federal Joint Committee (G-BA)
endpoints_required: Health-related quality of life (validated instruments, not restricted to EQ-5D)
Side effects (all-cause AEs, serious AEs, treatment discontinuation due to AEs)

endpoint_categories.patient_relevant: Mortality, morbidity, HRQoL, side effects — ONLY these count
endpoint_categories.surrogate_not_accepted: Surrogate endpoints NOT accepted unless validated link to patient-relevant endpoints
endpoint_categories.pfs_controversial: PFS alone is often NOT sufficient — must demonstrate link to OS or QoL

assessment_methodology.subgroup_analysis: IQWiG performs own subgroup analyses — pre-specified subgroups in protocol improve dossier quality
assessment_methodology.indirect_comparisons: Accepted only if no direct comparison available, using adjusted indirect comparison methods

dossier_requirements: Module 4: Health economic evaluation (for G-BA price negotiation)
Individual patient data access may be requested

common_gaps_in_protocols: Active comparator does not match G-BA's ACT (most critical gap)
Reliance on surrogate endpoints without validated link to patient-relevant outcomes
PFS as primary endpoint without OS or QoL co-primary
Missing quality of life data collection with validated instrument
No pre-specified subgroup analyses for German population
Missing side effect endpoints (all-cause AE rate, discontinuation rate)
Single-arm study design (no added benefit can be proven)
Composite endpoints that mix patient-relevant and surrogate components
recent_updates: 2025: Updated methodological guidance on indirect comparisons
2024: New framework for gene therapies and ATMPs
2024: Enhanced requirements for RWE in post-marketing studies

CADTH.full_name: Canada's Drug Agency (formerly Canadian Agency for Drugs and Technologies in Health)
CADTH.decision_framework: Deliberative framework with cost-effectiveness, patient values, clinical benefit

clinical_effectiveness.preferred_evidence: Systematic review with meta-analysis of RCTs
clinical_effectiveness.comparator: Canadian standard of care (may differ from US/EU)

cost_effectiveness.utility_measurement: EQ-5D with Canadian value set preferred
cost_effectiveness.drug_costs: Submitted price; pCPA negotiated price for final assessment

common_gaps_in_protocols: Comparator not reflecting Canadian standard of care
Missing EQ-5D data collection for utility estimation
No Canadian sites in multi-regional trial (external validity concern)
Insufficient follow-up for long-term cost-effectiveness extrapolation
Missing patient group input and patient values data

clinical_effectiveness.comparator: PBS-listed therapy or current clinical practice in Australia
endpoints_required: Clinical outcomes relevant to Australian clinical practice
Quality of life (preferably EQ-5D or AQoL)

cost_effectiveness.preferred_model: Matched to disease progression and clinical pathway
cost_effectiveness.utility_measurement: EQ-5D or AQoL (Australian Quality of Life)

common_gaps_in_protocols: No Australian QoL instrument (AQoL) data
Comparator not on PBS
Missing Australian healthcare resource use data
High discount rate means long-term benefits discounted heavily

AMNOG.note: AMNOG is the legislative framework; IQWiG conducts the assessment, G-BA makes the decision
AMNOG.decision_framework: Early benefit assessment within 3 months of launch

timeline: Day 0: Market entry at manufacturer's price
Month 9: G-BA decision on added benefit
Month 12: Price negotiation with GKV-SV completed

price_impact.no_added_benefit: Price capped at comparator therapy cost

common_gaps_in_protocols: Study comparator does not match G-BA's appropriate comparator therapy
Endpoints are surrogates rather than patient-relevant
No quality of life data collected
Missing mortality and morbidity endpoints
Study population does not match German treatment landscape

HAS.decision_framework: Dual assessment: clinical benefit (SMR) + improvement in clinical benefit (ASMR)

smr_rating.description: Service Médical Rendu — determines reimbursement eligibility

asmr_rating.description: Amélioration du Service Médical Rendu — determines price premium
asmr_rating.note: ASMR I-III allow premium pricing; ASMR IV modest premium; ASMR V price at comparator level

clinical_effectiveness.preferred_evidence: RCTs with active comparator reflecting French clinical practice
clinical_effectiveness.comparator: French standard of care as defined by CT; must match what French clinicians use
french_specific: Clinical benefit vs. existing therapies in French treatment landscape
Absolute benefit assessment (SMR), not just comparative (ASMR)
Public health interest assessment for population-level impact

economic_evaluation.when_required: CEESP assessment required for products claiming ASMR I-III or significant budget impact
economic_evaluation.perspective: Collective perspective (societal preferred, healthcare system accepted)
economic_evaluation.discount_rate: 2.5% for costs and outcomes (differs from NICE 3.5%)
economic_evaluation.utility_instrument: EQ-5D or HUI accepted; French tariff preferred

common_gaps_in_protocols: Comparator not matching French clinical practice (most common gap)
No quality of life data collection in clinical trial
Missing assessment of place in therapeutic strategy for France
Surrogate endpoints without clinical outcome validation
No French sites or French patient subgroup in MRCT
Insufficient long-term follow-up data for CEESP economic modelling

AIFA.decision_framework: Clinical value + budget impact + innovative therapy designation

innovative_therapy.description: Full Innovation designation provides automatic regional reimbursement + dedicated fund
innovative_therapy.benefit: Full Innovation: automatic listing on regional formularies, dedicated innovation fund (€1B/year)

clinical_effectiveness.preferred_evidence: RCTs demonstrating clinical benefit over available therapies in Italy
clinical_effectiveness.comparator: Italian standard of care (may differ from other EU countries)
endpoints_required: Overall survival or other hard clinical endpoints

managed_entry_agreements.description: Italy is a global leader in MEAs — most new drugs negotiated under some form of MEA

types.description: Full/partial refund if patient does not respond

managed_entry_agreements.registry: AIFA Monitoring Registry (Registri di Monitoraggio) required for MEA tracking
managed_entry_agreements.protocol_impact: Trials must define clear responder criteria to support outcome-based MEAs

common_gaps_in_protocols: No clear responder definition to support payment-by-results MEA
Missing early response biomarker for cost-sharing trigger
No Italian sites in MRCT (limits innovation designation)
Insufficient real-world evidence plan for registry monitoring
Comparator not available/standard in Italy

EU_JCA.effective_date: January 2025 (oncology and ATMPs first; all new active substances from 2030)
EU_JCA.decision_framework: Joint clinical assessment of relative effectiveness — pricing/reimbursement remains national

phase_3_from_2030: All new active substances and new therapeutic indications

mandatory: Description of the health condition and current clinical management
Relative clinical effectiveness vs appropriate comparator(s)
assessment_elements.not_included: Health economic evaluation, budget impact, pricing — remains at national level

evidence_requirements.comparator: Must address comparators relevant across EU member states — may need multiple comparators
evidence_requirements.population: EU-wide population; may need to address country-specific subgroups
methodological_standards: PICO framework mandatory (Population, Intervention, Comparator, Outcomes)
Joint scientific consultation available (replaces individual national consultations for JCA scope)

impact_on_protocol_design: Single comparator may not satisfy all EU member states — multi-arm or NMA strategy needed
Endpoints must be patient-relevant (surrogates challenged by IQWiG tradition will apply EU-wide)
QoL measurement mandatory from trial start
Subgroup analyses by EU-relevant factors (age, severity, prior treatment)
Evidence must be submitted in EU standardized JCA format
common_gaps_in_protocols: Single comparator that doesn't match all EU national standards of care
Reliance on surrogate endpoints without validated link to patient-relevant outcomes
No EU-wide QoL data collection strategy
Missing subgroup analyses relevant to diverse EU treatment landscapes
No plan for joint scientific consultation during protocol development

TLV.full_name: Tandvårds- och läkemedelsförmånsverket (Dental and Pharmaceutical Benefits Agency)
TLV.decision_framework: Value-based pricing with three principles: human value, need/solidarity, cost-effectiveness

cost_effectiveness.threshold: No formal threshold; ~SEK 500,000-1,000,000/QALY (~€45,000-90,000) typically accepted for severe diseases

clinical_effectiveness.comparator: Swedish clinical practice standard of care

HIRA.full_name: Health Insurance Review and Assessment Service
HIRA.decision_framework: Cost-effectiveness analysis with budget impact assessment

cost_effectiveness.threshold: GDP per capita per QALY (~₩40,000,000, ~$30,000 USD) informally applied

special_considerations: Risk-sharing agreements available for high-cost therapies
Korean patient subgroup data from MRCT preferred
PK/PD bridging data in Korean population may be requested

CONITEC.full_name: Comissão Nacional de Incorporação de Tecnologias no SUS
CONITEC.decision_framework: Cost-effectiveness from SUS (Unified Health System) perspective

clinical_effectiveness.preferred_evidence: Systematic review with meta-analysis of RCTs
clinical_effectiveness.comparator: Technology available in SUS (public health system)

special_considerations: Brazilian patient data or Latin American population representation valued
Public consultation process before final recommendation
Litigation risk: patients may access non-listed drugs via court orders (judicialização)

quality_of_life_instruments.description: QoL instruments accepted or preferred by each HTA body

EQ-5D-5L.notes: NICE prefers this; use country-specific value sets

EQ-5D-3L.notes: Mapping from 5L to 3L was standard practice; 2024 NICE update prefers 5L directly

SF-36_SF-12.notes: Can be mapped to EQ-5D; direct use for IQWiG

disease_specific.notes: Validated disease-specific PROs (e.g., EORTC QLQ-C30 for oncology) accepted by all bodies

comparator_alignment.description: Protocol comparator must align with each HTA body's standard of care definition
recommendations: Engage with HTA bodies early (Scientific Advice) to confirm appropriate comparator
Multi-arm trials that include HTA-relevant comparators where possible
Document standard of care by jurisdiction in protocol
Consider payer perspective when selecting control arm

health_economic_data_collection.description: Data that should be collected during clinical trials to support HTA submissions
must_collect: EQ-5D-5L at baseline and regular intervals
Healthcare resource utilization (hospitalizations, ER visits, outpatient visits)
Work productivity / caregiver burden (optional but valuable)
